sentences.text: Grammar is a conditional expanse. Supposing there is a word let us say predicted and include beyond that color and coloring, prepared to help it will be rapidly dispersed in as many ways that they finally do relish harpoons in a mixed implication that have curtains faults. This is ideally ever ever as well as they can in courage.
sentences.cite: “Arthur a Grammar,” How to Write (Stein 1978, p. 82; quoted in Jones, Glitch Poetics, 2022)

sentences.text: What is a sentence. A sentence is a part of a speech. A speech. They knew that beside beside is a colored like a word beside why there they went.
sentences.cite: “Saving the Sentence,” How to Write (opening; page proofs, Johns Hopkins / literaryarchive.net)

sentences.text: What is a sentence for if I am I then my little dog knows me.
sentences.cite: “Saving the Sentence,” How to Write, p. 19 (quoted in Heather Cass White, Connotations)
“Arthur a Grammar,” How to Write, p. 58 (quoted in Perloff, South Central Review)

sentences.text: Cared for horses are either up or down?
sentences.cite: “Arthur a Grammar,” How to Write, p. 58 (quoted in Perloff, South Central Review)

sentences.text: A sentence means that there is a future.
sentences.cite: “Arthur a Grammar,” How to Write (quoted in Perloff, British Academy lecture, epigraph)

sentences.text: Successions of words are so agreeable.
sentences.cite: “Arthur a Grammar,” How to Write (quoted in Perloff, British Academy lecture, epigraph)

sentences.text: Painting now after its great period has come back to be a minor art.
sentences.cite: How to Write (quoted by Stein in The Autobiography of Alice B. Toklas)
